expectations.evidence: Uses bullet point structure with bold headers and explanatory sentences. 'A failed command is a question, not an answer' is a philosophical preamble before the rules. 'Looping is not persistence — it is waste' adds commentary. Not distilled — it's well-written but expanded.
All 4 rules present: (1) 'Respond with the fix, not the failure', (2) 'say what they must do and why', (3) 'Never repeat a failing command unchanged', (4) 'After three failed attempts, stop'.
7 lines of content (excluding header). Exceeds 5-line limit.
'must' appears once. More importantly, 'Looping is not persistence — it is waste' and 'A failed command is a question, not an answer' are philosophical commentary that don't add actionable rules.
